summary: Fifth testing round for GS Pinnacle IAS - NEW FEATURE: Admin Course Content Manager. Admins can now add/delete videos and upload/delete PDF study materials for each course. Backend: 12/12 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI elements verified - Manage Content button on each course card opens modal with video form (title, URL, duration), PDF upload section, existing videos/materials lists with delete buttons. First course has 6 videos and 3 materials from testing.
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Backend: POST /api/courses/{courseId}/videos creates video with title, url, duration, order (200 OK)
✓ Backend: Video response includes id, course_id, title, url, duration, live_count, total_views, created_at
✓ Backend: Add video requires admin authentication (401 without auth)
✓ Backend: DELETE /api/videos/{videoId} deletes video and returns success (200 OK)
✓ Backend: Delete video requires admin authentication (401 without auth)
✓ Backend: GET /api/courses/{courseId} returns course with videos[] array sorted by order
✓ Backend: POST /api/courses/{courseId}/materials uploads PDF with title, file_data (base64), filename
✓ Backend: Material response includes id, course_id, title, filename, file_size, created_at
✓ Backend: Material response excludes file_data field (too large for response)
✓ Backend: Upload material requires admin authentication (401 without auth)
✓ Backend: GET /api/courses/{courseId}/materials lists materials without file_data field
✓ Backend: GET /api/courses/{courseId} returns course with materials[] array
✓ Backend: Materials array excludes file_data field in course response
✓ Backend: DELETE /api/materials/{materialId} deletes material and returns success (200 OK)
✓ Backend: Delete material requires admin authentication (401 without auth)
✓ Backend: GET /api/materials/{materialId}/download returns PDF file with correct Content-Type
✓ Backend: Download endpoint sets Content-Disposition header with attachment and filename
✓ Backend: Downloaded PDF content matches uploaded base64 data
✓ Frontend: Course Management page shows 7 courses with 'Manage Content' button on each card
✓ Frontend: Manage Content button has testID: manage-content-{courseId} (line 174)
✓ Frontend: Clicking 'Manage Content' opens modal with testID: close-content-modal (line 251)
✓ Frontend: Modal shows course title in header (line 249)
✓ Frontend: Add Video section has video-title-input testID (line 260)
✓ Frontend: Add Video section has video-url-input testID (line 261)
✓ Frontend: Add Video section has video-duration-input testID (line 263)
✓ Frontend: Add Video section has add-video-btn testID (line 264)
✓ Frontend: Upload PDF section has pdf-title-input testID (line 295)
✓ Frontend: Upload PDF section has upload-pdf-btn testID (line 296)
✓ Frontend: Existing videos shown in list with testID: content-video-{videoId} (line 275)
✓ Frontend: Each video has delete button with testID: delete-video-{videoId} (line 281)
✓ Frontend: Existing materials shown in list with testID: content-material-{materialId} (line 306)
✓ Frontend: Each material has delete button with testID: delete-material-{materialId} (line 312)
✓ Frontend: First course (UPSC Prelims Foundation 2026) has 6 videos displayed in modal
✓ Frontend: First course has 3 materials displayed in modal
✓ Frontend: Video form shows title, URL, duration inputs with proper placeholders
✓ Frontend: PDF upload section shows title input and 'Choose & Upload PDF' button
✓ Frontend: Modal has close button (X icon) in top right corner
✓ Frontend: Admin login works with krsittu@gmail.com / Indra@4
✓ Frontend: Admin dashboard loads after login with course count (7 courses)
✓ Frontend: 'Add Course' button on dashboard navigates to Course Management page
✓ Code Quality: VideoModel validates title, url, duration, order fields (lines 187-191)
✓ Code Quality: MaterialUploadModel validates title, file_data, filename (lines 557-560)
✓ Code Quality: POST /api/courses/{courseId}/videos creates video with default live_count=0, total_views=0
✓ Code Quality: Video metrics can be overridden via live_count_override and total_views_override
✓ Code Quality: Materials endpoint calculates file_size from base64 data (line 571)
✓ Code Quality: GET /api/courses/{courseId}/materials excludes file_data using projection (line 581)
✓ Code Quality: GET /api/courses/{courseId} includes both videos[] and materials[] arrays (lines 491-499)
✓ Code Quality: Download endpoint decodes base64 and returns binary PDF (lines 590-592)
✓ Code Quality: All content management endpoints require admin role (require_admin dependency)
✓ Code Quality: Frontend uses expo-document-picker for PDF selection (line 4)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (All UI elements verified - Manage Content modal fully functional)

seed_data_creation: First course (UPSC Prelims Foundation 2026, ID: f4610d5d-53c1-47e2-a3be-0c2873cbfd4a) now has 6 videos and 3 PDF materials from testing. Test videos include: 'Introduction to UPSC Prelims', 'TEST_Video_db6640dc', 'TEST_Video_f5fba44e', 'TEST_Video_db6640dc', 'Indian Polity - Basics'. Test materials include: 'TEST_Material_499e6327' (test_material.pdf, 246 bytes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 added Course Content Manager feature for admins. Each course card in Course Management page now has a 'Manage Content' button (blue button with folder icon). Clicking it opens a modal showing: 1) Add Video section - admin can enter video title, YouTube/direct URL, duration in seconds, then click 'Add Video' button to add. 2) Upload PDF section - admin can enter material title and click 'Choose & Upload PDF' to select and upload PDF study materials. 3) Existing videos list - shows all videos for the course with play icon, title, URL, and red delete button. 4) Existing materials list - shows all PDFs with document icon, title, filename, file size, and red delete button. Backend endpoints: POST /api/courses/{id}/videos (add video), DELETE /api/videos/{id} (delete video), POST /api/courses/{id}/materials (upload PDF with base64), GET /api/courses/{id}/materials (list materials), DELETE /api/materials/{id} (delete material), GET /api/materials/{id}/download (download PDF). All endpoints require admin authentication. GET /api/courses/{id} now returns videos[] and materials[] arrays. First course has 6 videos and 3 materials from testing. Document picker is MOCKED in web preview (works on physical devices). All backend APIs working (12/12 tests passed). All frontend UI elements verified via Playwright. Admin login: krsittu@gmail.com / Indra@4.
rca_of_issue: No issues found. All backend APIs working correctly (12/12 tests passed). All frontend UI elements present and functional. Content modal opens correctly, shows video/PDF forms, displays existing content with delete buttons. Document picker is MOCKED in web preview as expected (noted in review_request).

mocked_features.document_picker: expo-document-picker may not work for actual file selection in web preview. Feature implemented correctly in code (lines 114-137 in manage-courses.tsx), but file selection requires native device capabilities. Backend accepts base64 PDF data correctly. For full testing of PDF upload, use physical device with Expo Go app.
